stocks.name: Accenture plc
stocks.explanation: Accenture crashed to a 52-week low after reporting fiscal Q3 revenue of $18.72 billion, slightly missing estimates, and cutting the top end of its full-year revenue forecast. The company also announced $4.2 billion in cybersecurity acquisitions including Dragos, runZero, and NetRise. Investors are increasingly concerned that AI automation will erode demand for Accenture's traditional headcount-based consulting model, with the stock now down nearly 48% over the past year.

stocks.name: Marvell Technology Inc
stocks.explanation: Marvell surged on a trifecta of catalysts: the company will join the S&P 500 on June 22, triggering anticipated index fund buying; B. Riley raised its price target to $345 from $240 citing Nvidia collaboration strength; and Marvell named former Adobe finance chief Dan Durn as its new CFO. The stock is riding broader semiconductor momentum fueled by the Intel-Apple chip deal announcement.

stocks.name: Super Micro Computer Inc
stocks.explanation: Super Micro rallied alongside the broader semiconductor and AI infrastructure sector following Trump's Intel-Apple chip manufacturing announcement, which boosted sentiment across the entire chip supply chain. The server maker has been volatile in recent months after completing equity-linked financing transactions and navigating prior accounting concerns, but continues to benefit from AI data center demand.

stocks.name: Roblox Corporation
stocks.explanation: Roblox extended its recent rally after the global launch of its 'Kids' and 'Select' age-based account systems appeared to stabilize user engagement rather than hurt growth. TD Cowen highlighted a roughly 10% increase in average concurrent users over the weekend, easing fears that the company's child-safety push would permanently damage platform metrics. The stock is outperforming the broader tech market this week.

stocks.name: Micron Technology Inc
stocks.explanation: Micron surged ahead of its fiscal Q3 earnings report on June 24, with multiple analysts raising price targets — TD Cowen to $1,500, Citi to $1,200, and Aletheia Capital to $1,600. The memory chipmaker has guided for $33.5 billion in quarterly revenue with gross margins around 81%, driven by explosive HBM demand for AI training. Micron is up over 280% year-to-date and is now the third-best performer in the S&P 500.

stocks.name: Intel Corporation
stocks.explanation: Intel jumped after President Trump announced the company will partner with Apple to design and build chips in the United States. The deal would see Apple's M7 SoC manufactured on Intel's 18A-P process, targeting late 2027 mass production. Intel is up over 200% this year as its foundry turnaround gains credibility, though neither Apple nor Intel have formally confirmed the arrangement.

stocks.name: Infosys Limited
stocks.explanation: Infosys fell as IT services stocks sold off globally after the Federal Reserve signaled it could raise interest rates by year-end, dampening spending expectations. The Nifty IT index has underperformed the broader Indian market by 27% over six months. Like Accenture, Infosys faces growing concerns that AI automation will reduce demand for traditional IT outsourcing services.

stocks.name: KLA Corporation
stocks.explanation: KLA rallied as semiconductor equipment stocks broadly surged on the Intel-Apple domestic chip manufacturing announcement. Citi recently raised its price targets on KLA, Applied Materials, and Lam Research amid booming wafer fab equipment demand. Increased domestic chip manufacturing will require substantial capital equipment investment, directly benefiting companies like KLA.

stocks.name: Monolithic Power Systems Inc
stocks.explanation: Monolithic Power Systems rose alongside the broader semiconductor rally sparked by the Intel-Apple deal. The power management chipmaker has been a steady beneficiary of AI data center buildout, as its power delivery solutions are critical components in high-performance computing infrastructure. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index added as much as 5.4% on the session.

stocks.name: Applied Materials Inc
stocks.explanation: Applied Materials gained on the semiconductor equipment rally driven by Trump's Intel-Apple chip deal announcement. Citi recently lifted its price target on AMAT amid booming wafer fab equipment demand. As the world's largest semiconductor equipment maker, Applied Materials stands to benefit directly from expanded domestic chip manufacturing capacity.

stocks.name: ON Semiconductor Corp
stocks.explanation: ON Semiconductor rose with the broader chip rally following the Intel-Apple domestic manufacturing announcement. The company has been positioning itself as a key supplier for automotive and industrial semiconductor applications, and benefits from the renewed focus on U.S. chip manufacturing capacity and the broader Philadelphia Semiconductor Index surge of over 5%.

stocks.name: Space Exploration Technologies Corp
stocks.explanation: SpaceX fell for a second straight day as its post-IPO rally continues to cool. The stock surged 49% in its first three trading days after the largest IPO in history, briefly topping Amazon and Microsoft by market cap. Profit-taking has set in as the initial euphoria fades, though the company announced a $60 billion all-stock deal to acquire AI coding startup Cursor and added Sequoia's Roelof Botha to its board.

stocks.name: Wipro Limited
stocks.explanation: Wipro declined alongside Infosys and the broader Indian IT services sector after the Federal Reserve's hawkish signals pressured technology spending expectations. The Nifty IT index was the worst-performing sector, dropping amid fears that rising rates and AI-driven automation will compound headwinds for traditional IT outsourcing firms.

stocks.name: Lam Research Corporation
stocks.explanation: Lam Research climbed as semiconductor equipment stocks rallied broadly on the Intel-Apple chip deal. Citi recently raised its price target on Lam Research amid surging wafer fab equipment demand driven by AI buildout and domestic manufacturing expansion. Lam's etch and deposition tools are essential for manufacturing advanced logic and memory chips.

stocks.name: NXP Semiconductors NV
stocks.explanation: NXP Semiconductors rose with the broader chip sector rally following the Intel-Apple domestic manufacturing announcement. As a leading automotive and IoT semiconductor supplier, NXP benefits from the positive sentiment around expanded U.S. chip manufacturing and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index's 5.4% surge on the session.
